channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders and actions of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump began his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient U.S. government priorities. Some have an immediate policy impact. Others are more symbolic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's DEI regulations across government to 'backfire': Lawyer
item.description: President Trump slashed DEI programs, giving agencies 60 days to cut all positions and to proclaim there are only two genders.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Episcopal bishop says she'll continue to pray for Trump, who lashed out at her over viral sermon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Right Rev. Mariann Edgar Budde, bishop of the Diocese of Washington, said Wednesday that she will continue to pray for President Donald Trump, hours after he lashed out at her over a sermon. “I don’t consider him an enemy,” Budde told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at school. Some families are now weighing school attendance
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — As President Donald Trump cracks down on immigrants in the U.S. illegally, some families are wondering if it is safe to send their children to school.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, MORIAH BALINGIT, BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California enclave declares itself a 'non-sanctuary' city, will work with immigration officials, Trump admin
item.description: Lawmakers in Huntington Beach, California, voted Tuesday to become a "non-sanctuary" city in defiance of the state's sanctuary state law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House passes Laken Riley Act, sending it to Trump for first legislative win
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed the Laken Riley Act, sending the immigration-related bill to President Trump’s desk in what is poised to be his first legislative victory since returning to the White House this week. The chamber cleared the bill in a 263-156 vote.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's orders to end DEI programs reflect his push for a profound cultural shift
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s sweeping orders to end the government’s diversity, equity and inclusion effort mark a sea change for the country, unwinding decades-long priorities for the nation’s largest employer — the federal government — and broader efforts to push the private sector t
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steve Bannon's border wall trial delayed until March 4 as new lawyers plot aggressive defense
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon’s trial on charges that he duped donors who gave money to build a wall along the U.S. southern border will start a week later than scheduled, a judge said Wednesday, after the conservative rabble-rouser hired new lawyers to pursue an aggressive defense strategy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem who called Trump 'existential threat to democracy' now blocking his nominees
item.description: A Senate Democrat is blocking a streamlined process to vote on Trump's nominees, forcing the Senate to take lengthy procedural votes. This strategy was employed by Republicans while in the minority.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Where Donald Trump's executive orders rank historically
item.description: President Donald Trump is on pace to surpass Joe Biden's executive orders signed during his first 100 days in office
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Day One actions reversed Biden-era health policies, including efforts to expand ObamaCare
item.description: President Trump is rolling back Biden healthcare policies, such as expansions to the Affordable Care Act – a move Democrats described as an "attack" on the federal program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth lawyer slams 'flawed and questionable affidavit' from ex-sister-in-law
item.description: Pete Hegseth's lawyer dissected and refuted a new affidavit from the defense secretary nominee's former sister-in-law in a letter to SASC chairman Wicker on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace says she is considering a run for South Carolina governor
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Rep. Nancy Mace of South Carolina said Wednesday that she is “seriously considering” a gubernatorial run in 2026 and will make a final decision in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes visits Capitol Hill after Trump clemency
item.description: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes, the far-right extremist group leader convicted of seditious conspiracy in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack, has visited Capitol Hill after President Donald Trump commuted his 18-year prison sentence.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court could revive lawsuit against Texas officer who shot motorist stopped for unpaid tolls
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday seemed inclined to revive a civil rights lawsuit against the Texas police officer who shot a man to death during a traffic stop in Houston over unpaid tolls. The justices appeared to be in broad agreement that the New Orleans-based 5th U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon sending additional 1,500 troops to southern border: US official
item.description: The U.S. Pentagon is sending an additional 1,500 active-duty troops to the southern border by the end of the week, a senior U.S. military official told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles if California doesn't change water policies
item.description: President Donald Trump is threatening to withhold federal disaster aid for wildfire-ravaged Los Angeles unless California leaders change the state’s approach on its management of water.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump orders FAA to terminate DEI hiring efforts
item.description: "I hereby order the Secretary of Transportation and the Federal Aviation Administrator to immediately return to non-discriminatory, merit-based hiring, as required by law," the directive read.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to huddle with House GOP moderates at White House
item.description: A trio of moderate House Republicans are set to meet with President Trump at the White House on Wednesday, the latest huddle between the president and different GOP factions as the lower chamber prepares to pass his legislative agenda with its slim majority. Reps. Don Bacon (R-Neb.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Congressional Black Caucus blasts Trump DEI action as step backward
item.description: The Congressional Black Caucus (CBC), one of the largest in Congress, blasted President Trump on Wednesday for rolling back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardoned violent Jan. 6 rioters. Here are 5 of them
item.description: Hundreds of rioters accused of violently assaulting police at the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, were pardoned Monday by President Trump, despite remarks from his allies in recent weeks condemning those defendants.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom uses LA fires to criticize President Trump's reversal of Biden-era climate emissions standards
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom is attempting to criticize President Donald Trump's environmental executive orders by pointing to the recent deadly fires in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Working 'in tandem': Republicans prep to make Trump executive orders permanent
item.description: House Republicans are making sure a future Democratic administration cannot role back President Trump's signature executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde at the inaugural prayer service.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump rescinds Biden's executive order on AI safety in attempt to diverge from his predecessor
item.description: Hours after returning to the White House, President Donald Trump made a symbolic mark on the future of artificial intelligence by repealing former President Joe Biden’s guardrails for the fast-developing technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department orders investigation of local compliance with Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges against any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump defends pardoning of violent Jan. 6 offenders
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday defended signing mass pardons for rioters charged in connection with the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack, including those who were violent offenders, a move that has drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Adams says he won't publicly criticize President Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams said Tuesday he would not criticize President Donald Trump publicly. Adams also doggedly defended his decision to attend Trump’s inauguration Monday and skip local Martin Luther King Jr.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Plan to reform 300 Nevada boards, commissions emerges
item.description: After Gov. Joe Lombardo took aim in his State of the State address at an ever-expanding number of boards and commissions that operate in Nevada with minimal oversight, a plan to reform the system is emerging.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota and other states sue to stop Trump’s order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: Attorneys general from 22 states have sued to block President Donald Trump’s move to end a century-old immigration policy known as birthright citizenship guaranteeing that U.S.-born children are citizens regardless of their parents’ status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Cannon blocks DOJ from sharing Smith's Mar-a-Lago report with lawmakers
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon blocked the Justice Department from sharing with four top lawmakers the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report reviewing his investigation into President Trump’s mishandling of classified records.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5
